frameworks.name: drift diffusion model
frameworks.one_line: evidence accumulation framework capturing both choices and reaction times in trans-saccadic perception

frameworks.name: Bayesian observer
frameworks.one_line: alternative model that captured choices but failed to account for reaction-time signature

frameworks.name: saccadic suppression of displacement
frameworks.one_line: classical phenomenon motivating the study of trans-saccadic perceptual stability

core_question: How and when do external sensory signals bias perceptual judgments about object displacement following saccadic eye movements?
key_assumptions: perceptual stability requires integration of external sensory information with internal monitoring signals
choices and reaction times reflect a common underlying decision process amenable to sequential sampling models
manipulations: target displacement magnitude (up to ±2.5° along saccade trajectory)
key_findings: choices were attracted toward physically present sensory information
reaction times reflected an active, ongoing evidence accumulation process
Bayesian observer model captured choices but not reaction-time signature
drift rate scaled with sensory displacement magnitude
interpretations: only the DDM jointly accommodates choice bias and reaction-time dynamics in trans-saccadic perception
evidence accumulation actively continues across the saccadic eye movement
